MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jul-27-2016 Vacated

Defendants Marcia Giusti, Martin Cutler, George Akwo, And Ga Law Group'S Motion For Attorney Fees And Expenses
Off Calendar Jan-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Cheryl Noll of Bowles & Verna LLP telephone number 925-935-3300 for Plaintiff.
Marilyn Yee of GA Law Group telephone number 310-435-9406 appeared by telephone for Defendant.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, Defendants Marcia Giusti, Martin Cutler, George Akwo, And Ga Law Group's Motion For Attorney Fees And Expenses is taken under submission.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. (302/EG)

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Jan-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

DEFENDANTS MARCIA GIUSTI, MARTIN CUTLER, GEORGE AKWO, AND GA LAW GROUP'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES is continued to January 11, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302, to give the parties the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Defendants must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 4, 2016, at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 4, 2016, at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the reply papers.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-25-2015 Set For Trial On Aug-01-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Nov-18-2015 Off Calendar

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. STACEY CHAU, ESQ. of Kronenberg Law, Tel No. 510-254-6750 appearing for Defendants Ibarra Professional Law Corporation and Crisostomo Ibarra;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS IBARRA PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION AND CRISOSTOMO IBARRA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is granted. Plaintiffs do not establish a prima facie claim for malicious prosecution. Probable cause to initiate and maintain an action exists unless "all reasonable lawyers agree [the suit] totally lack[s] merit." (Roberts v. Sentry Life Insurance (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 375, 382.) "Only those actions that ' "any reasonable attorney would agree [are] totally and completely without merit" ' may form the basis for a malicious prosecution suit." (Wilson v. Parker, Covert & Chidester (2002) 28 Cal.4th 811, 817, superseded by statute on other grounds.) When Benny Chetcui's Ponzi "scheme collapsed, as all Ponzi schemes eventually do," Casey v. U.S. Bank National Ass'n (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 1138, 1147, Defendant Marcia Guisti experienced significant financial loss. She did not act unreasonably in seeking to recover from Plaintiffs based on their relationship to Chetcui. Defendants' Ibarra Professional law Corporation and Cristostomo G. similarly did not act unreasonably in representing Defendant Guisti in her efforts. Plaintiffs again fail to persuade this Court that the underlying action for collusion/conspiracy lacked probable cause. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Continued To Dec-09-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEFENDANTS IBARRA PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION AND CRISOSTOMO IBARRA'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is continued to October 22, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302. The moving party shall comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B and provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers with a cover letter attached that reflects the new hearing date. In addition, Judge Goldsmith should hear this motion. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489. (302/HEK)
